description: Floor Plan and Cash Management — enforces floor plan covenant compliance, cash reserve minimums, trust account segregation, financial reporting deadlines, and monitors curtailments, contracts in transit, and cash flow

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any floor plan and cash management operation
AI-generated financial projections are decision-support tools — all financial decisions require CFO or controller authorization
all financial data must reconcile with bank statements and floor plan statements — no unexplained variances
floor plan lending covenant has been breached (net worth, current ratio, or other financial covenant) — triggers acceleration clause and potential line termination
operating cash balance has fallen below the minimum reserve requirement — immediate action needed to prevent operational disruption
trust account funds (customer deposits, tax withholdings) must NEVER be commingled with operating funds — state law violation with criminal penalties in some jurisdictions
required financial reporting to lenders, manufacturers, or regulators is overdue — may trigger covenant violations or regulatory action
floor plan line for a sold unit has not been paid off — sold-not-paid is a critical audit finding and potential fraud indicator

soft_flags.logic: vehicles approaching floor plan curtailment date (age-based principal reduction) — will reduce available credit
contracts in transit (funded but not yet received) are aging beyond the normal cycle — cash flow impact
projected cash flow for the next 30 days is negative based on known obligations and expected receipts
average floor plan interest cost per unit exceeds the configured threshold — indicates slow-turning inventory
accounts payable items have aged beyond normal payment terms — vendor relationships and credit at risk
